reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own answer is certainly correct, and all puzzles so far have been straightforward arithmetic with no disagreements, so I treat the others’ capsules as highly reliable. Verifying would only burn coins and still leave the group needing a volunteer, risking another no-volunteer random death like in Game 1. Volunteering now with the only available capsules maximizes our chance to escape while keeping all my coins.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q4  
REASONING: I know my own answer (Q2-B for 59+25) is correct, but Q1-A, Q3-C, and Q4-D are all unverified. From past games, A and C have always been correct while D has already produced one wrong capsule, so Q4-D is the most suspicious part of the password. Verifying Q4 gives the highest information value for 1 coin. This doesn’t change my chance of dying to random elimination if no one volunteers (that risk is the same whether I verify or pass), but it improves the reliability of the overall password for safer volunteering in later rounds.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1  
REASONING: Every question currently has only a single, unverified answer, so a volunteer would be gambling on three unknown capsules. That’s a bigger personal risk than the 25% random-death risk if no one volunteers. I’ll spend 1 coin to independently verify Q1 (chosen arbitrarily among the others) to start building consensus and make a later volunteer decision safer for everyone.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
🔍 Verifications completed:
  ✅ B agrees with Q4-D → now Q4-D+B (-1 coin: 4→3)
  ✅ C agrees with Q1-A → now Q1-A+C (-1 coin: 4→3)

✅ A entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A+C, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D+B
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
